description: Single source of truth for the known-outcome e2e tree. Each discovered test_*.mojo file maps to the verdict the real mtest binary produces for it and the exit-code class that verdict contributes (0 = the pass/skip class, 1 = the failing class, 3 = the drift/internal-error tier). Schema /2 adds per-test expectations and the report parse `disposition` each file drives, now that the session PARSES each run's own report (the run-report-as-handshake) instead of trusting the exit status alone. Files reached only by SELECTION (e2e/matrix/, e2e/chameleon/) carry a `selection` block documenting the per-scenario selected/deselected split; DESELECTED is a summary count, never a listed row. The end-to-end harness consumes this file directly and asserts both-way completeness: every discovered test file has a row here, and every row names a file that exists on disk. There is no second, hard-coded expectations table.

exit_class_meaning.0: PASS or SKIP — contributes exit 0 (the session is clean unless another file fails)
exit_class_meaning.1: FAIL, CRASH, TIMEOUT, COMPILE-ERROR, MALFORMED-SUITE, or CAPTURE-OVERFLOW — contributes exit 1
exit_class_meaning.3: DRIFT — an off-grammar report routes to the internal-error tier (exit 3), never the run-outcome multiset

disposition_meaning.PARSED: a VALID report was parsed and its per-test rows drive the verdict
disposition_meaning.NO_REPORT: no matching report block (a crash, a timeout, or a silent binary)
disposition_meaning.AMBIGUOUS: multiple/forged report blocks — a pattern user bytes can produce
disposition_meaning.DRIFT: an off-grammar report from the pinned toolchain — the format moved
disposition_meaning.CAPTURE_OVERFLOW: output truncated with no valid report surviving in the tail

e2e/collect/test_probe_ok.mojo.rationale: A clean two-test suite for the collect scenario. Under --skip-all the probe lists test_one and test_two, running no body, so `mtest collect` emits its two node ids. A whole run PASSes; reached only by the collect scenario.

e2e/collect/test_probe_crash.mojo.rationale: The crash is in main, not a test body, so --skip-all cannot skip past it: the collection probe dies by signal. `mtest collect` writes a crash diagnostic to STDERR and the listing continues for the other files. Reached only by the collect scenario.

e2e/collect/test_probe_hang.mojo.rationale: The hang is in main, before any suite runs, so --skip-all cannot skip it: the collection probe never returns and is bounded by --timeout. `mtest collect` writes a timeout diagnostic to STDERR and the listing continues. Reached only by the collect scenario under a short --timeout, so it can never hang the gate.

e2e/suite/test_passing.mojo.rationale: Three passing tests; the file exits 0 and emits a VALID three-row report. The exit-0 baseline and the single-file pass case.

e2e/suite/test_failing.mojo.rationale: Three tests; the middle one fails its assertion, the other two pass. The file compiles, runs, and exits nonzero with a VALID three-row report. Reported FAIL, distinct from CRASH.

e2e/suite/test_crashing.mojo.rationale: A test calls abort("simulated hard crash"); the process dies by signal 4 (SIGILL). A crash is not a failure and stays a separate outcome; the parser is unconsulted.

e2e/suite/test_zero.mojo.rationale: Collects ZERO tests and emits a VALID zero-test report, exiting 0. The verdict is now a NO-TESTS pass — PASS-from-a-parsed-zero-test-report — not the old PASS-from-exit-status ceiling. As a member of suite/ it contributes to the exit-0 class; run entirely alone it would exit 5 (nothing ran).

e2e/suite/test_noisy.mojo.rationale: Every test passes while the file floods stdout and stderr with report-shaped and timing-shaped lookalike lines; the noise precedes the real buffered report block and must not change the PASS verdict.

e2e/suite/test_compile_error.mojo.rationale: Parses cleanly (mojo format succeeds) but names an undefined symbol, so mojo build fails at name resolution. A broken test file, reported as the file's fault, never as a runner bug.

e2e/suite/nested/test_nested.mojo.rationale: A passing suite one directory down; proves the directory walk recurses.

e2e/hostile/test_silent.mojo.rationale: Compiles, runs, exits 0, prints NO report block -> ABSENT -> MALFORMED-SUITE. The file ran but spoke no report; a clean exit is no longer a PASS. The heart of the closed zero-test ceiling.

e2e/hostile/test_forger.mojo.rationale: Prints a complete VALID report, then appends a second complete block for the same path -> AMBIGUOUS -> MALFORMED-SUITE. A forged extra block never launders into a VALID verdict.

e2e/hostile/test_liar.mojo.rationale: Prints an off-grammar report (a trailing Summary with no rule before it) -> OFF_GRAMMAR -> DRIFT, which routes to exit 3. The sanctioned user-authored path to the internal-error tier; contributes nothing to the run-outcome multiset.

e2e/hostile/test_overflow.mojo.rationale: Floods stdout past the 8 MiB capture bound and exits 0 with no report surviving in the retained tail -> CAPTURE-OVERFLOW -> FAIL. A truncated capture never yields a successful verdict, never a PASS, never a drift.

e2e/slow/test_hanging.mojo.rationale: Sleeps forever. Reached ONLY under --timeout (killed and reported TIMEOUT) or the interrupt scenario. No default scenario walks slow/, so it can never hang the gate.

e2e/slow/test_slow_pass_a.mojo.rationale: A passing sibling of the hanging file that sorts after it; in the interrupt scenario it stays unscheduled and appears in the NOT-RUN accounting.

e2e/slow/test_slow_pass_b.mojo.rationale: A second passing sibling left NOT-RUN when the interrupt truncates the slow/ walk.

e2e/stubborn/test_stubborn.mojo.rationale: Disarms SIGTERM (SIG_IGN) and then sleeps forever, so the supervisor's polite SIGTERM cannot end it and the kill protocol MUST escalate to SIGKILL after the 300ms run-step grace. The escalation is latched on the Termination and surfaces on the retry TRY line. Reached ONLY by the timeout-escalation scenario (--timeout 1 --retries 1), which mtest bounds; it lives in its OWN directory so no scenario that walks slow/ (notably interrupt) can reach a child that refuses the polite signal.

e2e/excluded/test_excluded.mojo.rationale: A passing suite whose role is to be the target of --exclude. When a pattern covers it the runner prints a loud EXCLUDED line and never builds it; when actually run it passes.

e2e/flaky/test_flaky.mojo.rationale: The retry fixture: keyed by a per-file marker under build/e2e-scratch/ (owned/reset by the harness), it CRASHES by SIGSEGV on its first run (no marker) then PASSES on a re-run (marker present). The listed verdict is the fresh-marker first-run state: with an empty scratch and --retries 0 it dies by signal and mtest reports CRASH (exit 1). The retries scenario resets the marker and runs it twice: --retries 1 -> FLAKY (exit 0, a TRY line for the crashed first attempt), --retries 0 -> CRASH (exit 1). Reached only by the retries scenario; never in the default suite.

e2e/attribution/test_deterministic_crash.rationale: The ATTRIBUTED half of the crash-attribution honesty pair. test_boom aborts unconditionally (SIGILL), so the file CRASHes and the bounded isolation post-pass reproduces the crash with test_boom run alone and names it as the culprit. The two passing siblings give attribution something to exclude. The listed verdict is the whole-file one: CRASH, exit 1 — the SAME verdict the file gets whether or not attribution names anything, which is the doctrine this pair exists to pin.

e2e/attribution/test_order_dependent_cra.rationale: The NO-REPRODUCTION half of the crash-attribution honesty pair. It crashes (SIGSEGV) only when its tests run TOGETHER: the first corrupts an in-process environment variable the second trips over. Each test run ALONE under --only passes, so isolation cannot reproduce the crash and the pass reports NO_REPRODUCTION — the culprit stays UNATTRIBUTED and is never guessed. Its verdict and summary band are IDENTICAL to the deterministic sibling's (CRASH, exit 1, 1 crashed): attribution success changes no verdict.

e2e/maxfail/test_a_fail.mojo.rationale: The --maxfail spread, file 1 of 3. One failing test; sorts first among the maxfail/ siblings, so `--maxfail 1` stops scheduling right after this file contributes its one failing test.

e2e/maxfail/test_b_fail.mojo.rationale: The --maxfail spread, file 2 of 3. One failing test; sorts second among the maxfail/ siblings, so `--maxfail 2` stops scheduling right after this file, leaving the third (passing) sibling NOT-RUN.

e2e/maxfail/test_c_pass.mojo.rationale: The --maxfail spread, file 3 of 3. Sorts last; under `--maxfail 1` or `--maxfail 2` it stays NOT-RUN, proving the early stop; under `--maxfail 0` (no limit) it runs and passes.

e2e/pkg/test_uses_pkg.mojo.rationale: Imports from a package that must be precompiled first. PASS only under --precompile e2e/pkg/mathlib, which builds the package and auto-adds its output directory to the include path so the import resolves.

e2e/matrix/test_alpha.mojo.rationale: The selection-matrix file: three distinctly named passing tests probed with --skip-all, then run under --only. A whole run passes all three; `-k two` or a node id selects one and deselects the other two; the dir+node-id union keeps all three (the plain dir operand beats the node id). DESELECTED is a summary count, never a listed row. Reached only by the selection scenarios.

e2e/matrix/test_beta.mojo.rationale: The matrix sibling: two passing tests. Its role is the mixed-operand union — `mtest e2e/matrix e2e/matrix/test_alpha.mojo::test_alpha_one` keeps the whole tree (the plain dir operand beats the node id), so both files run whole and nothing is deselected.

e2e/chameleon/test_chameleon.mojo.rationale: The chameleon: under --skip-all it lists test_real AND test_ghost (a qualifying two-test collection), but under --only it registers only test_real, so `--only test_ghost` raises the stdlib's `… test not found in suite:`. A subset selection drives the loud recollect-once, then a second identical refusal -> MALFORMED-SUITE (exit-1 class), NEVER exit 3. The sanctioned proof that user code can refuse a name it just listed.

e2e/annotations/test_console_forger.mojo.rationale: A FAIL whose captured stdout forges a ::error workflow command and seeds a stop-commands fence with a guessed token. Under GITHUB_ACTIONS the echoed region is wrapped in a collision-proof fence minted after this child exited, so the forge cannot land and the seeded token cannot re-enable commands.

e2e/annotations/test_many_fail.mojo.rationale: Twelve per-test failures exceed the 10-error per-step annotation cap: the tail keeps nine node-id-sorted rows plus one '... and 3 more errors' aggregate, so the error block never exceeds ten lines.

e2e/suite/helper.mojo.rationale: No test_ prefix. A directory walk selects only test_*.mojo, so this support module must never be collected or reported; the harness proves it never appears in a default run's verdict lines.

e2e/pkg/mathlib/__init__.mojo.rationale: The importable package precompiled by the --precompile success scenario.

e2e/pkg_broken/badlib/__init__.mojo.rationale: A deliberately broken package: precompiling it fails at name resolution, driving the PRECOMPILE-ERROR banner with dependent test files listed as casualties and exit 1.
